sections.paragraph:
, suff. מַֽחֲלֻקְתִּי, plur. מַחְלְקֹות (from the root חָלַק).

(1) smoothness, hence slipping away, flight (compare מָלַט), and so in pr.n. סֶלַע הַמַּחְלְקֹות the rock of escapings, 1Sa 23:28.

(2) an order, course, especially used of the twenty-four courses of the priests and Levites (ἐφημέριαι, κλῆροι), 1Ch 27:1, sqq.; 2Ch 8:14 31:2 35:4 .
